text.title: Writing Implementation Plans Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Writing Implementation Plans. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each impl-plan row equals exactly one task; tasks are not bundled.
rule.rationale: Each impl-plan row equals exactly one task; tasks are not bundled.

rule.statement: Sum of est_tokens across all tasks ≤ global cap.
rule.rationale: Sum of est_tokens across all tasks ≤ global cap.

rule.statement: Every task references at least one AD-X from design.md.
rule.rationale: Every task references at least one AD-X from design.md.

rule.statement: Every task carries an acceptance criterion that the worker pool can check binary.
rule.rationale: Every task carries an acceptance criterion that the worker pool can check binary.

rule.statement: Tasks are grouped into numbered waves; wave 0 has no deps; later waves depend only on earlier ones.
rule.rationale: Tasks are grouped into numbered waves; wave 0 has no deps; later waves depend only on earlier ones.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-writing-implementation-plans.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
